title: HAVE_LICHEN_LINK macro duplicates CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK check
description:
lichen_l2.c lines 28-35: Defines HAVE_LICHEN_LINK as 1 or 0 based on CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK, then uses #if HAVE_LICHEN_LINK throughout. The indirection adds no value - could use #if defined(CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK) directly.

However, the current pattern does enable writing 'if (HAVE_LICHEN_LINK)' in regular C code (not just preprocessor), which can be useful for avoiding #ifdef in function bodies. Mild style preference, not a bug.
close_reason: Taste: HAVE_LICHEN_LINK macro improves readability over raw CONFIG_ checks; minimal cost, no harm
